hypothesis: Applying a token-weighted KL-regularized DPO procedure—where the KL penalty is adaptively increased for domain-critical tokens (element symbols and multi-digit numerals) based on their SFT frequency—will prevent domain-token drift and, averaged across LLaMA-3.1-8B, Gemma-2-9B, and OLMo-2-7B, produce at least a 20% relative increase in the fraction of generated alloys satisfying all four synthesis objectives and at least a 10% relative increase in mean reward compared to standard DPO.
